title: calea pilosa
intro: Calea pilosa, also known as the Mexican tea or false dandelion, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Calea pilosa include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. Calea pilosa can be prepared as a tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, or it can be consumed as a supplement in capsule or powder form.
